target.name: Stellaria
target.founder: Nicolas Breton (CEO); backed by CEA + Schneider Electric

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stellaria, a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Nicolas Breton and backed by CEA and Schneider Electric, has raised €23M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises a universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Stellaria. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers to support this approach. Of the 8 private nuclear peers identified, filtering for stage alignment and valuation proximity yielded fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median triangulation. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology maturity, capital requirements, and deployment timelines across the peer set.

Without sufficient comparable transaction or trading data at seed stage within the nuclear sector, traditional quantitative valuation methods cannot be reliably applied to Stellaria. The analysis indicates that valuation support would require either (1) expansion to later-stage nuclear peers with acknowledged methodology caveats, or (2) application of cost-of-capital or DCF frameworks tied to company-specific technical and regulatory milestones. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity, capital efficiency relative to the peer set, and the funding environment for frontier nuclear technologies.
